channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Florida proposal would bar illegal migrants in the US from attending some colleges
item.description: A Florida state lawmaker has introduced a bill to ban illegal migrants from being admitted to public colleges and universities with an acceptance rate under 85%.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Oversight report says telework is 'wasting billions' in taxpayer cash ahead of 1st hearing
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: The House Oversight Committee found that prolonged pandemic-era telework has been “detrimental" to government agencies and new employee training.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol standoff spills into court as Minnesota Legislature gets off to rocky start
item.description: With accusations flying of which party is ignoring state law, the Minnesota Legislature’s session could be tinged with plenty of visits to the courthouse.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump attorney general nominee Pam Bondi to testify before Judiciary Committee
item.description: Trump's attorney general pick, Pam Bondi, to face questioning from the Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden promised nothing short of a national exorcism when he took office. He wanted to “restore the soul" of the country and prove that Donald Trump was only a footnote in the American story, not its next chapter.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson orders US Capitol flags raised to full height for Trump's inauguration
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson on Tuesday ordered that flags at the U.S. Capitol be raised to their full height on Inauguration Day, pausing a 30-day flag-lowering order following the death of former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada elections leader urges real-time efficiency over changing deadlines
item.description: The influx of mail-in ballots collected on Election Day led to delays in tabulation across Nevada, not the number of ballots received in the days after, Democratic Nevada Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar said ahead of the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP Sen. Joni Ernst officially backs Pete Hegseth for DOD after initial uncertainty
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst revealed she is officially supporting Pete Hegseth for Secretary of Defense after Tuesday's hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These 2 Democrats voted for House Republicans' bill to bar transgender athletes from girls sports
item.description: Reps. Vicente Gonzalez and Henry Cuellar, both of Texas, were the only Democrats to side with Republicans on Tuesday to pass legislation meant to bar transgender athletes from competing in girls and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Top DC prosecutor: Trump pardons couldn't erase impact of Capitol riot convictions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pardoning rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol four years ago can't erase the truth about what happened that day, the top federal prosecutor for Washington, D.C., said Tuesday as he prepares to leave office. “There is no undoing these prosecutions,” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House barrels toward fight over placing conditions on California fire aid
item.description: The House is barreling toward a contentious debate over disaster relief for California as many — but not all — Republicans call for conditions to be placed on the assistance, an idea Democrats are soundly rejecting. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin’s governor creates new violence-prevention office in wake of school shooting
item.description: Wisconsin’s Democratic governor on Tuesday created a new office dedicated to preventing gun violence, a month after a school shooting not far from the state Capitol and an idea that drew immediate opposition from Republicans who said it was misguided.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: CA lawmaker demands federal probe amid fire destruction, as Sacramento seeks unity and accountability
item.description: California State Assemblymembers spoke out on varying aspects of the LA wildfires and emergency preparedness, with one sending letters to Washington for investigatory help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem senator ripped for invoking Hegseth's young daughter in 'despicable' confirmation hearing exchange
item.description: SecDef nominee Pete Hegseth and Dem. Sen. Tim Kaine engaged in a tense back and forth about Hegseth's marital history and Hegseth's young daughter was mentioned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to ban transgender athletes from girls’ sports
item.description: House Republicans passed a bill Tuesday to bar transgender student-athletes from competing in girls’ sports, putting the contentious issue front and center following an election cycle that saw it become a frequent target of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama will skip Trump inauguration, but ex-Presidents Obama, Clinton and Bush will be there
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will skip the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, the second time in two weeks that she is not attending a gathering of former U.S. leaders and their spouses, but former Presidents Barack Obama, George W.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden moves to lift state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba, part of deal to free prisoners
item.description: President Joe Biden has notified Congress of his intent to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism, the White House announced, as part of a deal facilitated by the Catholic Church to free political prisoners on the island.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Aamer Madhani, Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he will create an 'External Revenue Service' agency to collect tariff income
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday announced plans to create a new agency called the External Revenue Service to collect tariffs and other revenues from foreign nations.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Kathy Hochul delivers State of the State address in NY
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – During her State of the State address, Gov. Kathy Hochul proposed free tuition for older college students, more police on New York City's subways overnight and tax relief for New Yorkers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota House DFLers boycott opening session; Republicans elect their own speaker
item.description: DFLers on Tuesday made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo as the parties fight over control. House Republicans pushed ahead anyway in the half-empty chamber. It’s all likely headed to the courts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. faces a complicated confirmation with some opposition on both sides of the aisle
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s views on vaccines and abortion rights are raising alarms across the political spectrum, as he's one of the more controversial picks for the president-elect's Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo raises $3.4 million in 2024 for re-election bid
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is off to a strong start in building campaign funds, raising about $3.4 million over the past year, according to mandatory campaign filing reports.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Special counsel in Hunter Biden case denounces president for criticism of the probe
item.description: In a final report on his investigation, special counsel David Weiss said President Biden's criticism "undermines the very foundation of what makes America's justice system fair and equitable.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: How to watch Trump's inauguration on NewsNation
item.description: Donald Trump will be inaugurated as the 47th U.S. president on Jan. 20 for his second nonconsecutive term.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis's call for special session 'premature,' Senate President, House Speaker say
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: DFL, GOP jockey for power in Minnesota House a day before session’s start
item.description: House Republicans ripped DFLers over tactics that could keep the House from conducting business Tuesday. Melissa Hortman, the House’s top DFLer, defended her moves, telling MPR News that denying a quorum is “the only negotiating power I have right now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Judge who blocked release of Trump report was 'plainly' wrong, special counsel tells appeals court
item.description: The Justice Department has asked a federal appeals court to move swiftly in reversing a judge’s order that had blocked the agency from releasing any part of special counsel Jack Smith’s investigative report on Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis open to special session for immigration, hurricane relief
item.description: It’s widely known that Gov. Ron DeSantis is open to the idea of a special session to help resolve the condo affordability crisis. However, the governor is also open to the idea of state lawmakers doing more before the yearly scheduled session starts.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC
